proof: function[T0, T2](x0: List[T0], x1: (T0, T2) -> T2, x2: T2) { List.nil[T0] != x0 or fold_right[T0, T2](x0, x1, x2) = x2 }[T, U](List.nil[T], f, init)

goal: fold_right[T, U](List.cons(item, List.nil[T]), f, init) = f(item, fold_right[T, U](List.nil[T], f, init))

proof: function[T0, T1: Monoid](x0: List[T0], x1: T0 -> T1) { fold_right[T0, T1](x0, fold_right_monoid_step[T0, T1](x1), T1.1) = fold_right_monoid[T0, T1](x0, x1) }[T, A](List.cons(head, tail), f)

goal: step(head, fold_right[T, A](tail, step, A.1)) = f(head) * fold_right[T, A](tail, step, A.1)

goal: p(items)
proof: not forall(x0: T, x1: List[T]) { not p(x1) or p(List.cons(x0, x1)) } or forall(x2: List[T]) { p(x2) } = true
function(x0: List[T]) { not forall(x1: T, x2: List[T]) { not p(x2) or p(List.cons(x1, x2)) } or p(x0) }(items)
let w0: T satisfy { exists(k0: List[T]) { p(k0) and not p(List.cons(w0, k0)) } }
let w1: List[T] satisfy { p(w1) and not p(List.cons(w0, w1)) }
let w2: List[T] satisfy { p(w2) and not p(List.cons(w0, w2)) }
function(x0: List[T], x1: T) { not p(x0) or p(List.cons(x1, x0)) }(w2, w0)
